hero.title: Track, Train, and Climb Together
hero.subtitle: One app for every climbing board

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.p1: Kilter, Tension, MoonBoard, Decoy, Grasshopper. Every board has its own app and its own walled garden. Your training shouldn't be locked inside one of them.
vision.p2: Boardsesh works across all of them so you can just climb.

features.title: What Boardsesh Offers
features.queueDescription: Take turns without the awkward "who's next?"
features.partyDescription: Share a session and climb together in real time
features.multiBoardDescription: Works with Kilter, Tension, MoonBoard, and more
features.communityDescription: Built and improved by people who actually climb
features.selfHostedDescription: Run your own instance if that's your thing

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.body: Boardsesh is open source under the Apache license. Browse the code, send a PR, file a bug, or fork the whole project.

api.title: API Documentation
api.body: Want to build on climbing data? The API is public. Have at it.

community.title: Join the Community
community.body: Whether you write code, set problems, or just want to tell us what's broken, we'd like to hear from you.

about.footer: Made by climbers. Open to everyone.

intro.title: Legal & Intellectual Property Policy
intro.p1: Boardsesh is a free, open-source, community-built alternative for browsing and interacting with standardised LED climbing training boards (Kilter Board, Tension Board, MoonBoard, and others). It is non-commercial and maintained entirely by volunteers.
intro.p2: We believe climbers should have the freedom to choose how they interact with hardware they've purchased, and that community-created data should remain accessible to the community.

facts.title: Climbs are factual data
facts.p1: A climb on a standardised board is a list of hold positions on a fixed, standardised layout (e.g. "start on A5, use B12, C3, D18, finish on K11"), combined with a grade and wall angle. This is factual, functional information — comparable to a chess position, a set of GPS coordinates, or a phone number — not a creative work of authorship.
facts.p2: Under U.S. copyright law, facts are not copyrightable (Feist Publications, Inc. v. Rural Telephone Service Co., 499 U.S. 340 (1991)). A standardised board with a finite number of holds (typically 200–500) produces climb definitions that are inherently constrained, factual, and functional.

community.title: The database is community-created
community.p1: The climb databases for these boards are almost entirely user-generated content. Individual climbers create and submit climbs. The board manufacturers did not author this content — they provide a platform for submission and display.
community.p2: The licensing position varies by manufacturer, but in no case does a manufacturer own the community's climb data:
community.aurora: Aurora Climbing (Kilter Board, Tension Board, and others): Aurora's Terms of Use grant them a "perpetual, unrestricted, unlimited, non-exclusive, irrevocable license" over user-submitted content. Crucially, this license is non-exclusive — it does not transfer ownership to Aurora, nor does it prevent the original creator (or third parties) from independently using, collecting, or presenting the same factual data.
community.moonBody: As of the last review of Moon Climbing's public-facing legal documents (February 2026), Moon Climbing has no app-specific Terms of Service, no End User License Agreement, and no contributed content license governing the MoonBoard app or its climb database. In the absence of any such agreement, Moon Climbing has no contractual claim over climbs created and submitted by its users.

compilation.title: Comprehensive databases have weak compilation protection
compilation.body: Even where a database arrangement might qualify for thin copyright protection as a compilation, the U.S. Supreme Court has held that protection extends only to creative selection or arrangement — not to the underlying facts. A database that comprehensively collects all user submissions without editorial curation (as these platforms do) is analogous to an alphabetical phone directory, which the Court found unprotectable in Feist.

attribution.title: Attribution & Respect for Creators
attribution.intro: We respect the climbing community and the people who create climbs.
attribution.item1: Where climb creator information is available, we attribute the creator by their username.
attribution.item2: We do not claim authorship of climbs we did not create.
attribution.item3Start: If you are the creator of a climb and would like it removed, please
attribution.item3End: or contact us and we will honour your request promptly.

interop.title: Interoperability & Hardware Compatibility
interop.intro: This project provides software and hardware designs that are compatible with standardised climbing board hardware. Climbers who have purchased these boards have a right to use third-party software and controllers with their own hardware.

software.title: Software interoperability
software.body: Our app communicates with board controllers over standard Bluetooth protocols. The Bluetooth communication protocol used by these boards is unencrypted and based on commodity hardware (WS2812B addressable LEDs driven by standard Bluetooth controllers). Implementing a compatible Bluetooth interface for the purpose of interoperability is well-established as lawful under both U.S. and EU law (Sega Enterprises Ltd. v. Accolade, Inc., 977 F.2d 1510 (9th Cir. 1992); EU Directive 2009/24/EC, Article 6).

controller.title: Open-source controller
controller.p1: This project also offers open-source controller hardware and firmware designs. The controller is an independent, original implementation that communicates using the same Bluetooth protocol as the official controllers. It is built on commodity components (standard microcontrollers and WS2812B LEDs) and does not incorporate any proprietary firmware, code, or hardware designs from any board manufacturer. No patents are known to exist covering the LED controller systems used by any current board manufacturer.
controller.p2: Our controller is designed to be interoperable with both this project's software and, at the user's discretion, official manufacturer apps. Any such interoperability is initiated by the end user on their own hardware.

trademark.title: Trademark Usage
trademark.body: We use board and product names (e.g. "Kilter Board", "MoonBoard", "Tension Board") solely to describe hardware compatibility and interoperability. These names are trademarks of their respective owners. This project is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Aurora Climbing, Moon Climbing, or any board manufacturer.

dmca.title: DMCA & Takedown Requests
dmca.intro: We take intellectual property concerns seriously. If you believe any material in this project infringes your copyright, please contact us with the following information:
dmca.item1: Identification of the copyrighted work you believe is infringed.
dmca.item2: Identification of the material you believe is infringing, with enough detail for us to locate it.
dmca.item3: Your contact information (name, address, email, phone number).
dmca.item4: A statement that you have a good faith belief that the use is not authorised by the copyright owner, its agent, or the law.
dmca.item5: A statement, under penalty of perjury, that the information in your notice is accurate and that you are the copyright owner or authorised to act on their behalf.
dmca.review1: We will review all valid requests promptly and in good faith. We may also file a counter-notice where we believe a takedown request is based on a misidentification of the material or a misunderstanding of the law.

community.title: A Note to the Community
community.p1: The climbing community has a long tradition of openly sharing beta. This project exists in that spirit. We are not trying to harm any company — we are trying to give climbers better tools to interact with hardware they already own and data they helped create.
community.p2: Manufacturers continue to benefit from this ecosystem. Our project drives demand for their physical products (holds, boards, panels) where they provide genuine value. We simply believe climbers deserve a choice in how they interact with their hardware.
community.p3: If you are a board manufacturer and would like to discuss collaboration or have concerns, we welcome open dialogue. Please reach out.

legal.footer: This document is provided for informational purposes and does not constitute legal advice. Last updated: 08-02-2026

hero.title: How can we help?
hero.subtitle: Learn about Boardsesh features and get the most out of your climbing sessions

heatmap.q: How do I use the heatmap feature?
heatmap.intro: The heatmap shows hold usage patterns across all climbs, helping you identify popular holds and training opportunities.
heatmap.access1: Open the search drawer and expand the "Holds" section
heatmap.access3: The board will display a color overlay from green (low usage) to red (high usage)

modes.ascentsBody: Overall popularity based on total completions

heatmap.outro: Use the toggle switches to show/hide hold numbers and include/exclude foot holds.

holdClassification.q: How do I classify holds on my board?
holdClassification.intro: Hold classification lets you create personal ratings for each hold on your board, helping you understand your strengths and find suitable climbs.

attrs.holdTypeBody: Jug, Sloper, Pinch, Crimp, or Pocket

holdClassification.outro: Classifications are personal and stored in your account, allowing you to build a customized understanding of your board over time.

partyMode.q: How do I start a collaborative session?
partyMode.intro: Party Mode allows multiple climbers to share a queue and take turns on the board in real-time.
partyMode.start1: Tap the lightbulb icon in the queue bar at the bottom
partyMode.start2: Go to the "Start Session" tab and sign in if not already logged in
partyMode.start3: Click "Start Party Mode" to create a new session
partyMode.start4: Share the session with friends using QR code or link

join.qrBody: The session host can show a QR code that others can scan with their phone camera
join.shareBody: Copy and share the session URL - anyone with the link can join
join.idBody: Go to the "Join Session" tab and paste either the full URL or just the session ID

partyMode.feature1: Real-time queue synchronization across all devices
partyMode.feature3: Session leader controls the board display
partyMode.feature4: Automatic leader handoff if the current leader disconnects
partyMode.feature5: Session persists across page refreshes and reconnections

queueManagement.q: How do I manage the climb queue?
queueManagement.intro: The queue lets you organize climbs for your session, whether climbing solo or with others.

actions.addBody: Double-tap any climb in the list to add it to your queue
actions.reorderBody: Drag and drop climbs to change order
actions.currentBody: Click a climb to make it the active climb
actions.removeBody: Click the X on any queued climb
actions.mirrorBody: Toggle mirror mode for bilateral training

queueManagement.outro: The queue syncs automatically with party members and persists offline for uninterrupted sessions.

playlistGenerator.q: How do I generate a workout playlist?
playlistGenerator.intro: The Playlist Generator creates structured workouts tailored to your training goals.

types.volumeBody: High repetitions at a consistent grade for endurance
types.pyramidBody: Ramp up to a peak grade, then back down
types.gradeFocusBody: Concentrated work at a single target grade

mirroring.q: How do I mirror a climb?
mirroring.intro: Climb mirroring flips all holds to the opposite side of the board, perfect for bilateral strength training.
mirroring.step1: Find the climb you want to mirror
mirroring.step2: Look for the mirror toggle icon in the climb actions
mirroring.step3: Toggle it on to flip the climb horizontally
mirroring.outro: When connected via Bluetooth, the LED board will automatically display the mirrored pattern. This feature is available on supported boards like Kilter Homewall.

searchByHold.q: How do I search by specific holds?
searchByHold.intro: Search by Hold lets you find climbs that use (or avoid) specific holds on the board.
searchByHold.step1: Open the search drawer and expand the "Holds" section
searchByHold.step2: Select "Include" or "Exclude" mode from the dropdown
searchByHold.step3: Tap holds on the board to select them
searchByHold.step4: Results update automatically as you select holds
searchByHold.includeBody: Climbs must use the selected holds
searchByHold.excludeBody: Climbs must not use the selected holds

bluetooth.q: How do I connect my board's LEDs?
bluetooth.intro: Boardsesh uses Web Bluetooth to control your board's LED system directly from the browser.
bluetooth.req1: Chrome browser (recommended) or other Web Bluetooth-compatible browser
bluetooth.req2: iOS users: Use the Boardsesh app (Safari doesn't support Web Bluetooth)
bluetooth.req4: Board powered on and in range
bluetooth.connect1: Tap the lightbulb icon in the queue bar
bluetooth.connect2: Select your board from the device list
bluetooth.connect3: Once connected, LEDs automatically show the current climb
bluetooth.outro: The connection supports mirrored patterns and will automatically update when you change climbs.

userSync.q: How do I sync with my Kilter/Tension account?
userSync.intro: Link your Aurora account (Kilter or Tension) to sync your climb history and progress.
userSync.link1: Open the menu (tap your avatar) and go to Settings
userSync.link3: Enter your Kilter or Tension credentials
userSync.outro: Changes sync bidirectionally, so ascents logged in Boardsesh will appear in the official apps.

logbook.q: How do I track my climbs?
logbook.intro: Track your climbing progress by logging ascents and attempts.

actions.tickBody: Record an attempt on the climb

logbook.outro: Your logbook syncs with linked Aurora accounts and can be filtered in search results to find new challenges or revisit old projects.

offline.intro: Yes! Boardsesh is designed to work reliably even with intermittent connectivity.
offline.works1: Your queue persists locally via IndexedDB
offline.works2: Session tokens are cached for quick reconnection
offline.outro: When connectivity is restored, any pending changes sync automatically in the background.

docs.headerSubtitle: Complete reference for the Boardsesh REST and WebSocket APIs

overview.title: Boardsesh API Overview
overview.intro: Boardsesh provides two complementary APIs for interacting with interactive climbing training boards (Kilter, Tension):

rest.title: REST API
rest.intro: Use the REST API for stateless operations like searching climbs, fetching board configuration, and user authentication. The REST API is ideal for:

ws.title: WebSocket GraphQL API
ws.intro: Use the WebSocket API for real-time features like party sessions and queue synchronization. The GraphQL API provides:
ws.feature4: All REST API functionality via queries/mutations

auth.title: Authentication
auth.restBody: Uses NextAuth session cookies. Authenticate via
auth.wsBodyEnd: and include it in the connection parameters:

rateLimit.title: Rate Limiting
rateLimit.body: Authentication endpoints are rate-limited to prevent abuse. Rate limit headers are included in responses. Public read endpoints have generous limits.

ws.title: WebSocket Connection Guide
ws.introStart: The Boardsesh WebSocket API uses the

session.title: Session Management
session.body: Sessions are the core collaboration unit. Users join sessions to share a climb queue.

delta.title: Delta Synchronization
delta.body: When reconnecting, use delta sync to catch up without full state transfer:

connectionHandling.title: Connection Handling
connectionHandling.body: The WebSocket connection may be interrupted by network changes. Implement reconnection logic with the graphql-ws retry options and use delta sync to maintain state consistency.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro1: Boardsesh is an open-source app for controlling climbing training boards (Kilter, Tension, MoonBoard) over Bluetooth. This policy explains what data we collect, why, and what we do with it.
privacy.intro2: The short version: we collect only what we need to make the app work. We do not sell your data, we do not run ads, and we do not use third-party tracking.

collect.title: What we collect
collect.accountBody: When you create an account, we store your email address and username. Your email is used for authentication. Your username is displayed on your profile and visible to other users in Party Mode sessions and social features.
collect.activityBody: When you log a send or attempt, we store the climb details, grade, date, and any notes you add. This powers your logbook and progression tracking.
collect.locationBody: If you grant location permission, we use your approximate location to help you discover nearby Party Mode sessions. We do not track your location in the background or store location history.
collect.analyticsBody: We use Vercel Analytics to collect anonymous page view and performance data. This helps us understand which features are used and where the app is slow. This data is not linked to your account.

bluetooth.title: Bluetooth
bluetooth.body: Boardsesh uses Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) to connect to your climbing board's LED controller. The Bluetooth connection is used exclusively to send hold lighting commands to the board. No personal data is transmitted over Bluetooth. The app does not read data from the board beyond basic device identification during pairing.

location.title: Location data
location.body1: Location access is optional. When granted, it is used for one purpose: making your Party Mode session discoverable to nearby climbers. Your location is shared with the server only while a Party Mode session is active. We do not store your location history, build a location profile, or share location data with anyone outside of the party session discovery feature.
location.body2: You can revoke location permission at any time in your device settings. The app works fully without it.

thirdParty.title: Third-party services
thirdParty.vercelBody: We use Vercel for hosting and analytics. Vercel processes requests to serve the app and collects anonymous usage metrics. See
thirdParty.auroraBody: If you link your Aurora Climbing account (used by Kilter and Tension boards), we sync your climb data with Aurora's servers so your logbook stays consistent across apps. This sync only happens when you initiate it. See

sharing.title: Data sharing
sharing.body1: We do not sell your data. We do not run ads. We do not share your information with third-party marketers or data brokers. The only data shared externally is what is described above (Vercel hosting/analytics, Aurora Climbing sync when you opt in).
sharing.body2: Other Boardsesh users can see your username, profile, and logged climbs if you participate in social features like Party Mode. This is visible in the app and is part of how the product works.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.body: We keep your account data and climb history for as long as your account is active. If you stop using Boardsesh, your data stays until you delete your account.

deletion.title: Account deletion
deletion.intro: You can delete your account and all associated data at any time. There are two ways to do it:
deletion.inAppBody: Go to Settings, scroll to the bottom, and tap Delete Account. You will be asked to confirm before anything is removed.
deletion.onWebBodyEnd: and scroll to the bottom of the page to find the same Delete Account button.
deletion.permanent: Deletion is immediate and permanent. Once confirmed, we remove your email address, username, logbook entries, queue data, and party session history from our servers. This cannot be undone.

children.title: Children
children.body: Boardsesh is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe a child under 13 has created an account, contact us and we will delete it.

changes.title: Changes to this policy
changes.body: If we change this privacy policy, we will update this page with the new text and the "last updated" date. For significant changes, we may also notify you in the app.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body1Start: Questions about this policy or your data? Email us at
contact.body2Start: Boardsesh is open source. You can see exactly what data the app collects and how it is processed by reading the source code on
